Register your school teams for the MindSpark Science and Mathematics Quiz—an engaging, concept-driven competition for students in Grades 8, 9 and 10.

Before you begin

Basic instructions

Please review the competition format, eligibility and technical requirements.

01

Concept-first questions

Questions cover high-school Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry and Biology, with an emphasis on understanding and application.

02

Beyond rote learning

The quiz evaluates deep conceptual clarity and the ability to apply ideas to unfamiliar situations.

03

Open across syllabi

Questions are not tied to one board. They are appropriate for students in Grades 8, 9 and 10.

04

Three students per team

Each team must have exactly three students, from any combination of Grades 8, 9 and 10.

05

Student email required

Email addresses are used only for login credentials, test links, study material and competition communication.

06

Learning resources

After registration and fee confirmation, students receive sample questions, online content and competition guidelines.

07

Registration fee

The fee is ₹1,800 per team. The total is calculated automatically based on the number of teams selected.

08

Elimination round

The written online elimination round is on 8 August 2026. A phone or computer with internet access is required.

09

Qualification update

Qualified teams will be informed by 12 August 2026, along with guidance for the next rounds.

10

Interactive finals

Semi-final and final rounds are online and interactive. Teams must participate from school using a camera, microphone and internet connection.
